homepage.heroSubline: Honest tire services for Louisville drivers. We know Kentucky roads and weather demand reliable tires.
aboutText: Summit Tire Shop opened its doors in Louisville with one goal: to provide straightforward tire services without the upsells. Founded by two mechanics tired of seeing customers overcharged for simple repairs.
We believe in showing you exactly what your vehicle needs. Our technicians explain options clearly, whether you're driving through Highlands' historic streets or commuting to St. Matthews.
As Louisville grows, we're committed to keeping our community rolling safely. We sponsor little league teams and donate tires to local vocational schools.

valueProps.title: Local knowledge
valueProps.description: We understand how Kentucky's seasonal changes affect tire wear and performance.

valueProps.title: Fair pricing
valueProps.description: No hidden fees - just honest assessments and competitive rates.

valueProps.title: Quick turnaround
valueProps.description: Most services completed same-day so you're back on the road faster.

valueProps.title: Quality brands
valueProps.description: We carry trusted names that hold up to Louisville's potholes and weather extremes.

homepage.ctaHeadline: Need tires that handle Louisville roads?
homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-TIR-R91 today for honest advice from our team.

services.name: Tire Replacement
services.shortDescription: Expert tire selection and installation for Louisville's varied driving conditions.
services.description:
Louisville's mix of urban streets, highway commutes, and seasonal weather demands the right tires. Our technicians help you choose between all-season, performance, or specialty tires based on your driving habits.

We inspect your current tread wear pattern to identify potential alignment issues before installing new tires. All installations include proper torque checks and road testing. For homes near limestone-heavy areas like Old Louisville, we recommend tires with reinforced sidewalls to handle uneven surfaces.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive inspection of current tires and suspension
Step 2: Discussion of driving needs and budget considerations
Step 3: Professional mounting and balancing using Hunter equipment
Step 4: Final inspection and test drive
benefits: Extended tread life through proper installation
Improved handling on Louisville's varied road surfaces
Better fuel efficiency from properly balanced tires

faq.question: How often should Louisville drivers replace tires?
faq.answer: Most tires last 3-5 years here. Summer heat accelerates wear, while winter potholes can cause sudden damage. We recommend seasonal inspections.

faq.question: What's the best tire type for commuting to Churchill Downs?
faq.answer: For mixed city/highway driving, we recommend all-season touring tires with good wet traction for spring storms.

services.name: Flat Tire Repair
services.shortDescription: Reliable flat fixes that get you back on Louisville roads quickly.
services.description:
From nails in NuLu to curb damage in St. Matthews, we handle most flat tire repairs in under an hour. Our technicians first determine if the puncture is repairable based on its location and size - we never patch unsafe damage just to make a sale.

Louisville's limestone substrate means many roads develop sharp edges that can pinch tires. We inspect the inner liner for hidden damage even when the puncture seems straightforward. All repairs include rebalancing to prevent vibration issues on I-65 or Watterson Expressway.
process: Step 1: Locate puncture using water test and visual inspection
Step 2: Remove tire and inspect interior for secondary damage
Step 3: Apply industry-approved patch/plug combination
Step 4: Rebalance wheel assembly
benefits: Includes free pressure check for other tires

faq.question: Can you repair sidewall damage?
faq.answer: Never. Sidewall compromises require replacement for safety. We'll explain why and show you the damage.

faq.question: Do you offer mobile flat repair?
faq.answer: Yes, for simple flats within 10 miles of our shop. Complex cases may need tow-in service.

services.name: Wheel Alignment
services.shortDescription: Precision alignments to combat Louisville's pothole season.
services.description:
Kentucky's freeze-thaw cycles create alignment-challenging road conditions each winter. Our Hunter alignment system precisely measures camber, caster, and toe angles to manufacturer specifications.

We pay special attention to vehicles frequently crossing the Big Four Bridge or navigating downtown's brick streets. The process includes a pre-alignment inspection of suspension components - we'll notify you of any worn parts affecting alignment accuracy. Proper alignment improves tire life by 20-30% on Louisville's roads.
process: Step 1: Test drive to identify handling characteristics
Step 2: Suspension component inspection
Step 3: Four-wheel alignment measurement
Step 4: Adjustment and verification

faq.question: How often should Louisville drivers get alignments?
faq.answer: Annually, or immediately after hitting a major pothole. Spring is ideal after winter road damage.

faq.question: Can alignment fix my car pulling to one side?
faq.answer: Usually yes, but we first check for other causes like brake drag or tire pressure differences.

services.name: Tire Rotation
services.shortDescription: Regular rotations extend tire life on Louisville's varied road surfaces.
services.description:
Front-wheel drive vehicles in Louisville wear front tires faster due to stop-and-go traffic and turning demands. Our rotation service follows manufacturer patterns while inspecting for developing wear issues.

We document tread depth at each rotation to help predict replacement timing. The service includes checking for uneven wear that might indicate alignment needs - common after driving Highlands' angled streets. Most Louisville drivers should rotate every 5,000-7,000 miles depending on their commute routes.
process: Step 1: Record current tread depths per tire
Step 2: Remove and reposition tires per rotation pattern
Step 3: Torque lug nuts to spec
Step 4: Reset TPMS sensors if needed

faq.question: Why rotate if I'll replace all tires at once?
faq.answer: Regular rotations prevent premature replacement of just two tires when others still have life.

faq.question: Do you rotate directional tires?
faq.answer: Yes, we follow proper procedures for directional and staggered fitments common on performance vehicles.

services.name: TPMS Service
services.shortDescription: Keep your tire pressure monitoring system working through Kentucky's temperature swings.
services.description:
Louisville's 50-degree daily temperature variations in spring and fall frequently trigger false TPMS alerts. We diagnose whether your warning light indicates actual low pressure or a sensor issue.

Our shop can program new sensors to match your vehicle's system after tire replacements. We also replace faulty valve stem sensors - a common failure point on Louisville vehicles exposed to road salt. Proper TPMS function is especially important for drivers commuting to bourbon country on winding rural roads.
process: Step 1: Physical pressure check against TPMS reading
Step 2: Sensor signal strength test
Step 3: Battery voltage check
Step 4: Reprogramming or replacement as needed

faq.question: Why does my TPMS light come on when temperatures drop?
faq.answer: Tire pressure decreases about 1 PSI per 10°F. We'll check if your pressure is truly low or just temperature-affected.

faq.question: Can you disable my TPMS?
faq.answer: No, it's a safety system required by law. We'll fix it properly instead.

services.name: Winter Tire Services
services.shortDescription: Prepare for Kentucky's icy bridges and sudden snowstorms.
services.description:
While Louisville winters are relatively mild, black ice on the Ohio River bridges and sudden snow squalls demand proper traction. We help determine if winter tires make sense for your commute - particularly valuable for healthcare workers and other essential personnel.

Our winter package includes seasonal changeovers with pressure adjustments for cold weather, plus dedicated storage for your off-season tires. We recommend winter tires for drivers regularly visiting hilly areas like Cherokee Park or southern Indiana. All installations include TPMS checks to ensure proper cold-weather monitoring.
process: Step 1: Assess your driving needs and patterns
Step 2: Recommend appropriate winter tire options
Step 3: Professional mounting and balancing
Step 4: Storage setup for off-season tires

faq.question: Are winter tires worth it in Louisville?
faq.answer: For drivers crossing bridges daily or working essential jobs, yes. Casual drivers may manage with quality all-seasons.

faq.question: When should I switch to winter tires?
faq.answer: Ideally before Thanksgiving, as our first freeze often surprises drivers in late November.

services.name: Custom Wheel Installation
services.shortDescription: Proper fitment for Louisville's car enthusiasts and practical upgrades.
services.description:
From classic car owners in Old Louisville to truck drivers wanting better looks and performance, we ensure proper wheel fitment. Our technicians verify backspacing, offset, and load capacity to prevent rubbing or suspension issues.

We specialize in maintaining TPMS functionality during wheel upgrades - important for Louisville's inspection requirements. The process includes road testing to confirm no interference at full lock, especially valuable for vehicles navigating tight downtown streets or parking garages. All installations use hub-centric rings when needed for vibration-free highway driving.
process: Step 1: Verify vehicle specifications and owner goals
Step 2: Test fitment before mounting tires
Step 3: Professional mounting with proper equipment
Step 4: Road test and adjustment

faq.question: Will larger wheels affect my ride quality?
faq.answer: Generally yes, but we can recommend tire combinations to minimize harshness on Louisville roads.

faq.question: Do you offer wheel repair for curb damage?
faq.answer: Yes, we can refinish most alloy wheels damaged by Louisville's tight parking spots.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: The team helped me choose the perfect tires for my commute from NuLu to the Ford plant. Noticed my old tires had uneven wear and fixed my alignment issue too. Six months later, tread wear is perfectly even.

reviews.name: Janelle K.
reviews.text: Got a nail in my tire near Churchill Downs right before derby week. Summit had me back on the road in 45 minutes with a proper patch job. They showed me exactly where the damage was and why it was safe to repair.

reviews.name: Derrick S.
reviews.text: Appreciated their honest advice about whether I needed winter tires for my Crosstrek. Ended up getting them since I drive to Indiana daily. The storage service makes seasonal swaps hassle-free.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: After hitting a massive pothole on Eastern Parkway, my car pulled hard to the right. Summit's alignment fixed it completely and showed me the before/after measurements. No sales pressure for unneeded suspension work.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: My sensor kept giving false alarms every cold morning. They diagnosed a weak battery and replaced just that one sensor instead of pushing for all four. Fair pricing and fast service.

reviews.name: Emily W.
reviews.text: As a Highlands resident with street parking, I appreciate their quick rotations while I wait. They always point out any developing wear patterns from our angled streets.
